SEARCH
Package com.icarus.events

Class OrganizerCreateEventTest

java.lang.Object
com.icarus.events.OrganizerCreateEventTest
public class OrganizerCreateEventTest extends Object
Comprehensive instrumented UI tests for OrganizerCreateEventActivity.

This suite exercises the full event-creation flow via Espresso, from entering form data through to verifying persisted Firestore documents. Each test redirects writes to a temporary Firestore collection (managed by FirestoreCollections.startTest() / FirestoreCollections.endTest()) so that production data is never affected.

User Stories Covered

  • US 02.01.04 – As an organizer, I want to set a registration period (open and close dates).
  • US 02.03.01 – As an organizer, I want to optionally limit the number of entrants who can join my waiting list.
  • US 02.02.03 – As an organizer, I want to enable or disable the geolocation requirement for my event.

Key Design Decisions

  • Scroll-before-interact pattern: every view interaction calls scrollTo() (or the internal scrollFieldIntoView(int) helper) before acting on a view. The entire form lives inside a NestedScrollView; without scrolling first, Espresso dispatches key events to whichever view currently holds focus — frequently the nav-bar search field or a completely different input — silently corrupting test data.
  • Geolocation dialog must be fully driven: the confirm button validates that a map marker has been placed and the radius SeekBar is non-zero before dismissing the dialog. Any test that enables the geolocation switch must call interactWithGeolocationDialog() to drive the dialog to completion, otherwise all subsequent Espresso interactions will time out waiting for the dialog to disappear.
  • Firestore latch timeout: every CountDownLatch waiting on a Firestore result uses a 15-second timeout so that a missing document fails fast rather than stalling the test runner indefinitely.
  • Callback assertion capture: AssertionError instances thrown inside Firestore callbacks are flagged via an assertionFailed[] boolean and re-checked on the test thread, ensuring JUnit registers the failure correctly.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    cleanup()
    Tears down the test environment after each test method.
    void
    setup()
    Prepares the test environment before each test method.
    void
    testAllFieldsAcceptInput()
    Smoke test: verifies that every interactive control on the event-creation screen accepts input without throwing, resetting, or routing input to the wrong field.
    void
    testEventCreatedWithoutWaitingListLimit()
    Verifies that leaving the optional capacity field empty creates an event whose Firestore capacity field is null.
    void
    testGeolocationDisabled()
    Verifies that leaving the geolocation switch in its default-off state stores geolocation = false and a null coordinates field in Firestore.
    void
    testGeolocationEnabled()
    Verifies that enabling the geolocation switch and completing the map dialog stores geolocation = true and a non-null coordinates GeoPoint in Firestore.
    void
    testImagelessEventCreationSavedToFirestore()
    End-to-end test: fills every form field, omits an event poster, submits the form, and asserts that the resulting Firestore document contains the correct values for all persisted fields.
    void
    testImageUploadFromDevice()
    Verifies the full image-upload path: selecting a poster from the device gallery triggers a Cloudinary upload, and the resulting Firestore event document stores a non-empty image URL.
    void
    testLocationIsCapitalisedBeforeSaving()
    Verifies that the activity normalises the location string before saving it to Firestore: the first character is uppercased and all remaining characters are lowercased, regardless of the casing entered by the user.
    void
    testOrganizerIdStoredInCreatedEvent()
    Verifies that the current user's ID is included in the organizers array of the Firestore event document created by the activity.
    void
    testPrivateEventDisabled()
    Verifies that leaving the private-event switch in its default-off state stores isPrivate = false in the Firestore event document.
    void
    testPrivateEventEnabled()
    Verifies that toggling the private-event switch on stores isPrivate = true in the Firestore event document.
    void
    testSelectedImageDisplayedInPosterView()
    Verifies that selecting a gallery image causes the poster ImageView to display content other than the default placeholder.
    void
    testValidationBlocksSubmitWhenDatesAreMissing()
    Verifies that submitting the form without any dates selected triggers activity-level validation and does not write a document to Firestore.
    void
    testValidationBlocksSubmitWhenRequiredTextFieldsEmpty()
    Verifies that submitting the form with name, category, and location all empty triggers activity-level validation and does not write a document to Firestore.
    void
    testWaitingListLimitStoredCorrectly()
    Verifies that the numeric value entered in the capacity field is persisted exactly in the Firestore capacity field.

  • Constructor Details

    • OrganizerCreateEventTest

      public OrganizerCreateEventTest()

  • Method Details

    • setup

      public void setup()
      Prepares the test environment before each test method.

      Specifically, this method:

      1. Initialises the Cloudinary MediaManager with test credentials (silently ignored if already initialised).
      2. Calls FirestoreCollections.startTest() to redirect all Firestore reads and writes to a temporary test collection, protecting production data.
      3. Seeds UserSession with a minimal User whose ID is "dummyOrganizerId" so that the activity can resolve the current organizer without a real sign-in flow.

    • cleanup

      public void cleanup() throws Exception
      Tears down the test environment after each test method.

      In order:

      1. Closes the ActivityScenario if one was opened.
      2. Deletes any Cloudinary asset whose URL was captured in uploadedEventImageUrl, along with its Firestore images document, to avoid accumulating orphaned data.
      3. Calls FirestoreCollections.endTest() to restore the default (production) Firestore collection name.
      4. Resets all shared-state fields to null so that a partially failed test cannot bleed state into the next one.
      Throws:
      Exception - if the Cloudinary or Firestore cleanup requests fail

    • testAllFieldsAcceptInput

      public void testAllFieldsAcceptInput()
      Smoke test: verifies that every interactive control on the event-creation screen accepts input without throwing, resetting, or routing input to the wrong field.

      Controls exercised:

      • Event title, description, location, and capacity text fields.
      • Category spinner (selects the first non-placeholder option).
      • Geolocation toggle — the full map dialog is driven to completion (place marker → set radius → confirm) because leaving it open would block all subsequent interactions.
      • Private-event toggle clicked on then off, confirming it returns to its default state.
      • All four date/time pickers via selectDates().

      This test does not submit the form or assert Firestore state; it is solely a UI-layer sanity check.

    • testImagelessEventCreationSavedToFirestore

      public void testImagelessEventCreationSavedToFirestore() throws InterruptedException
      End-to-end test: fills every form field, omits an event poster, submits the form, and asserts that the resulting Firestore document contains the correct values for all persisted fields.

      Assertions cover:

      • Text fields: name, description, and location (which the activity normalises to title-case).
      • Numeric capacity stored as a double.
      • Boolean defaults: both geolocation and isPrivate must be false when neither switch is toggled.
      • Timestamp day-of-month: each of the four date fields must match the day constant selected in the picker (REG_START_DAY, REG_END_DAY, EVENT_START_DAY, EVENT_END_DAY).
      • Chronological ordering: registration open < close <= event start < event end.

      User Stories: US 02.01.04 (registration period), US 02.03.01 (waiting-list limit).

      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if the Firestore CountDownLatch is interrupted before the query completes

    • testImageUploadFromDevice

      public void testImageUploadFromDevice() throws Exception
      Verifies the full image-upload path: selecting a poster from the device gallery triggers a Cloudinary upload, and the resulting Firestore event document stores a non-empty image URL.

      The system image-picker intent (Intent.ACTION_GET_CONTENT) is stubbed with Espresso Intents so no real gallery UI appears. A synthetic 50×50 PNG created in the app cache and served through FileProvider acts as the picker result.

      Because Cloudinary uploads are asynchronous, the test polls Firestore for up to 30 seconds, checking once per second until the image field is populated. This avoids hard-coded sleeps while still bounding the worst-case wait time.

      Throws:
      Exception - if the test image cannot be created, the Cloudinary upload fails, or the Firestore latch is interrupted

    • testSelectedImageDisplayedInPosterView

      public void testSelectedImageDisplayedInPosterView() throws Exception
      Verifies that selecting a gallery image causes the poster ImageView to display content other than the default placeholder.

      This is a pure UI-layer check. The form is not submitted and Firestore is not inspected. The test confirms only that the activity calls ImageView.setImageURI() (or equivalent) with the URI returned by the stubbed picker, resulting in a non-null drawable.

      Throws:
      Exception - if the synthetic test image cannot be created or the FileProvider URI cannot be resolved

    • testGeolocationEnabled

      public void testGeolocationEnabled() throws InterruptedException
      Verifies that enabling the geolocation switch and completing the map dialog stores geolocation = true and a non-null coordinates GeoPoint in Firestore.

      The geolocation switch launches a full-screen map dialog that requires three steps before its confirm button will dismiss it:

      1. Tap the MapView to place a marker at the tapped location.
      2. Wait ≥ 500 ms for OSMDroid's GestureDetector to fire onSingleTapConfirmed (internally delayed ~300 ms to distinguish a single tap from a double-tap).
      3. Click the radius SeekBar at 50 % of its width so that onStopTrackingTouch sets selectedEntrantRange to a non-zero value.
      All three steps are handled by interactWithGeolocationDialog(). Skipping any of them causes the confirm button to display a Toast and refuse to close the dialog, timing out all further Espresso interactions.

      User Story: US 02.02.03 – enable geolocation requirement.

      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if the Firestore CountDownLatch or the dialog-setup sleep is interrupted

    • testGeolocationDisabled

      public void testGeolocationDisabled() throws InterruptedException
      Verifies that leaving the geolocation switch in its default-off state stores geolocation = false and a null coordinates field in Firestore.

      User Story: US 02.02.03 – disable geolocation requirement.

      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if the Firestore CountDownLatch is interrupted before the query completes

    • testWaitingListLimitStoredCorrectly

      public void testWaitingListLimitStoredCorrectly() throws InterruptedException
      Verifies that the numeric value entered in the capacity field is persisted exactly in the Firestore capacity field.

      User Story: US 02.03.01 – optionally limit the waiting list.

      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if the Firestore CountDownLatch is interrupted before the query completes

    • testEventCreatedWithoutWaitingListLimit

      public void testEventCreatedWithoutWaitingListLimit() throws InterruptedException
      Verifies that leaving the optional capacity field empty creates an event whose Firestore capacity field is null.

      The field must not silently default to zero, as a capacity of zero would incorrectly bar all entrants from joining the waiting list.

      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if the Firestore CountDownLatch is interrupted before the query completes

    • testPrivateEventEnabled

      public void testPrivateEventEnabled() throws InterruptedException
      Verifies that toggling the private-event switch on stores isPrivate = true in the Firestore event document.
      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if the Firestore CountDownLatch is interrupted before the query completes

    • testPrivateEventDisabled

      public void testPrivateEventDisabled() throws InterruptedException
      Verifies that leaving the private-event switch in its default-off state stores isPrivate = false in the Firestore event document.
      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if the Firestore CountDownLatch is interrupted before the query completes

    • testValidationBlocksSubmitWhenDatesAreMissing

      public void testValidationBlocksSubmitWhenDatesAreMissing() throws InterruptedException
      Verifies that submitting the form without any dates selected triggers activity-level validation and does not write a document to Firestore.

      The test sleeps for 2 seconds after tapping submit to confirm that no asynchronous Firestore write occurs during that window. This is intentionally conservative: the activity's validation guard should return synchronously before any write is attempted.

      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if the sleep or Firestore latch is interrupted

    • testValidationBlocksSubmitWhenRequiredTextFieldsEmpty

      public void testValidationBlocksSubmitWhenRequiredTextFieldsEmpty() throws InterruptedException
      Verifies that submitting the form with name, category, and location all empty triggers activity-level validation and does not write a document to Firestore.

      Dates are selected to ensure the test reaches the text-field validation branch rather than being blocked earlier by the date check.

      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if the sleep or Firestore latch is interrupted

    • testLocationIsCapitalisedBeforeSaving

      public void testLocationIsCapitalisedBeforeSaving() throws InterruptedException
      Verifies that the activity normalises the location string before saving it to Firestore: the first character is uppercased and all remaining characters are lowercased, regardless of the casing entered by the user.
      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if the Firestore CountDownLatch is interrupted before the query completes

    • testOrganizerIdStoredInCreatedEvent

      public void testOrganizerIdStoredInCreatedEvent() throws InterruptedException
      Verifies that the current user's ID is included in the organizers array of the Firestore event document created by the activity.

      The expected ID ("dummyOrganizerId") matches the value seeded into UserSession by setup().

      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if the Firestore CountDownLatch is interrupted before the query completes
